mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand as the discount rate floor is reached, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is always-on when the trigger condition is met.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=0.1% indicates maximum accommodation regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market slack and reduced wage pressure, which lowers corporate cost inflation expectations. This environment supports equity valuations and produces positive forward returns over 60 trading days as market reprices growth and margin risks downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE=6.1% indicates slack labor market
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 5.5% are not significantly positive (mean return ≤ 0% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) remain below 1.2M annualized units, signaling weakening consumer demand. This dual contraction in demand proxies predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS=439891M, HOUST=1001K suggests demand softness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month below the prior 3-month trend, indicating supply-side contraction. This signals reduced economic capacity utilization and compressed profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices cyclical earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO=103.66 requires trend comparison for trigger validation
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > -0.5% are positive in more than 65% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling labor market deterioration. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices recession risk and forward earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100K AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS=138780K, UNRATE=6.1% requires trend validation
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when both conditions are triggered, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5% while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2.0%, indicating real rates are negative. This regime of negative real rates supports equity valuations through financial repression, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL=237.083, FEDFUNDS=0.1% indicates potential negative real rate regime
regime_hint: financial repression with negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in negative real rate regimes are not significantly positive (mean ≤ 0.5% or t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously contract month-over-month. This dual demand weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession probability and forward earnings.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1=15824.2B, RSAFS=439891M requires growth rate validation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of instances when both demand indicators weaken, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows above 1.0% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously increases above 150K new jobs per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and labor productivity gains. This dual supply strength predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 1.0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150K
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO=103.66, PAYEMS=138780K requires growth rate validation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both supply indicators strengthen, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
